text: Subject: Updated farm checklist for review.
I attach the checklist. Please reply.
Hi Carlos. You write to Jean in Rwanda today. What is your subject line?
My subject is: Updated farm checklist for review.
Good. Jean is a colleague, so start with Hi Jean. Now the body.
I attach the checklist and ask for feedback before the deadline.
Perfect. And how do you ask him to answer?
Could you confirm that you receive the file? Please reply to this email.
I look forward to your reply. Best regards, Carlos.
I hope this email finds you well.
I attach the updated farm checklist.
Could you confirm that you receive the file?

Hi Carlos, this is Emma. Let me read you a simple model email, part by part. First, the subject: Updated farm checklist for review. Then the greeting: Hi Jean. Then the body: I hope this email finds you well. I attach the updated farm checklist. Please review it and send me your feedback. The deadline is next Friday. Could you confirm that you receive the file? Finally, the sign-off: I look forward to your reply. Best regards, Carlos. That is all. A subject, a greeting, a body, and a sign-off.

Hi Carlos, a quick note about writing emails at Sucafina. To a colleague like Jean in Rwanda, Hi is fine, and you can be direct. To the Swiss headquarters, Dear is more formal, and you write with more care. Always add a clear subject line, so the reader knows the topic. Always attach the file before you send. And always end with Best regards and your name. Keep it short and polite. Good luck with your first email.

First, Carlos writes the subject line. Then he writes the greeting: Hi Jean. Then he writes the body and attaches the checklist. He asks Jean to confirm. Finally, he ends with Best regards and sends the email.
I attach the updated farm checklist.
Could you confirm that you receive the file?
I hope this email finds you well.
I look forward to your reply. Best regards, Carlos.
